data.title: Cryptocurrency trading: investment or gambling?
data.description:
In September 2025, the Journal of Financial and Quantitative Analysis published an article titled "Gambling on Crypto Tokens?". This paper used Google Trends as a proxy variable for retail investor interest and found that regions with a high gambling propensity showed stronger interest in both fungible and non-fungible (NFT) crypto tokens. The study indicated that this interest was significantly higher during crypto market bubbles and for lottery-like tokens. Furthermore, retail investor interest in cryptocurrencies declined after the legalization of sports betting. The study further found a positive correlation between increased token interest and increased participation and larger fundraising amounts. However, after periods of high interest, consumer credit default rates surged, and this phenomenon was limited to subprime borrowers. In summary, this research suggests that gambling preference is an important indicator for predicting retail investor interest in the crypto market.

I. Introduction

Since the advent of Bitcoin in 2009, the crypto market has experienced explosive growth. During this period, thousands of crypto tokens—digital assets created on blockchains (a decentralized, distributed digital ledger)—have emerged. These tokens can represent a variety of assets and uses: well-known examples such as Bitcoin and Ethereum are primarily used as a medium of exchange or a store of value, while other crypto tokens can be used to acquire specific products or services on the blockchain platform, or represent ownership of physical and digital items. Along with the market expansion, consumer interest has also grown, with over 20% of U.S. adults having invested in, traded, or used crypto tokens (CNBC (2022)), and an estimated 580 million crypto investors worldwide (Crypto.com (2024)).

Despite the significant growth in the retail crypto investor population, direct evidence regarding the characteristics of these investors is scarce due to the anonymity inherent in blockchain technology. Meanwhile, the surge in crypto investors has raised concerns among policymakers, particularly given the extreme volatility of the crypto market. For example, the total market capitalization of crypto reached nearly $2.8 trillion in November 2021, then plummeted to $1.2 trillion in June 2022 before rebounding to $2.6 trillion in May 2024 (Forbes (2024)). This dramatic volatility raises concerns that retail investors may not fully understand the associated risks. Specifically, crypto token returns exhibit a highly positive skewness, meaning there is a tiny probability of obtaining extremely high returns (Liu and Tsyvinski (2021), Liu, Tsyvinski, and Wu (2022)). This return pattern resembles lottery products and is highly attractive to investors with a strong gambling preference (Kumar (2009)). Therefore, this paper explores whether gambling preferences can predict retail investor interest in the crypto market. Understanding whether retail investors view crypto tokens as lottery-like products can help policymakers determine appropriate disclosure standards and regulatory frameworks (such as the legislative recommendations made by Lummis and Gillibrand (2023)). In the absence of direct data, this paper draws on the work of Da, Engelberg, and Gao (2011), using Google search interest as a proxy variable for retail investor interest, focusing on two significant types of crypto tokens: Initial Coin Offerings (ICOs) and the non-fungible token (NFT) series. Unlike tokens as general-purpose currencies, ICOs focus on project investment, while NFTs focus on digital ownership and collectibles. Consistent with the view that gambling preferences predict crypto interest, this paper finds significantly higher interest in crypto tokens in regions with higher per capita lottery sales. This finding is also robust to other gambling-related demographic characteristics identified by Kumar (2009) and Kumar, Page, and Spalt (2011). To alleviate concerns that "attention does not equal investment," this paper documents that attention to crypto wallets surged both before and after token issuance, with higher attention correlated with larger fundraising amounts and more participants. Furthermore, this paper excludes other explanatory paths such as advertising, risk appetite, or distrust of the system. This paper further explores the token hierarchy factors influencing gambling-driven attention. First, ICO and NFT projects launched during crypto market bubbles attract more attention in regions with high gambling propensity. Second, in the ICO market, tokens with lower opening prices (i.e., more "lottery-like" characteristics) and a lack of Know Your Customer (KYC) protocols (Li, Shin, and Wang (2021)) also generate greater interest in these regions. Furthermore, using the gradual legalization of sports betting in various US states as a natural experiment, this paper finds that attention to token issuance relatively decreased in regions with high gambling propensity after legal sports betting was permitted. This suggests that crypto tokens are largely seen as alternatives to traditional gambling products by retail investors. Finally, this paper examines the relationship between crypto attention and consumer credit consequences. Using data from Equifax, this paper finds that consumer credit default rates surged in regions with high gambling propensity following periods of high crypto attention, and this phenomenon was concentrated among financially constrained subprime borrowers. Lag analysis shows that the increase in attention preceded the rise in default rates. This paper contributes to several areas of literature: First, it provides new perspectives on the characteristics and motivations of retail investors in ICO market research (Li and Mann (2025), Lee and Parlour (2021), Cong, Li, and Wang (2021, 2022), etc.); Second, it enriches the NFT literature, revealing the relationship between retail investor attention and primary market performance (Kong and Lin (2021), Borri, Liu, and Tsyvinski (2022), Oh, Rosen, and Zhang (2023)); Third, it expands the literature on the impact of gambling preferences on the prices and trading volumes of financial products (Barberis and Huang (2008), Bali, Cakici, and Whitelaw (2011), Kumar (2009), Green and Hwang (2012)); Fourth, it connects research on retail investor behavior (Barber and Odean (2000, 2008), Welch (2022), Fedyk (2022), Barber et al. (2022)); Finally, this paper supplements emerging literature on the characteristics of crypto investors (Dhawan and Putniņš (2023), Hackethal et al. (2022), Kogan et al. (2024), Aiello et al. (2023), Divakaruni and Zimmerman (2024), Sun (2023)), demonstrating that gambling preference is an important factor in predicting retail investor interest in the crypto market. This section introduces the data sources used in the study and the descriptive statistics of the variables in the regression analysis. A. Data Sources 1. Retail Investor Attention This paper adopts the method of Da et al. (2011), using online attention obtained from Google Trends as a proxy variable for investment behavior. Its advantage lies in capturing investors' search intentions in a private environment. The study uses the Google Search Volume Index (SVI), with a value range of 0–100. This paper collects data at a more granular level of Specific Market Regions (DMAs), covering 209 DMAs in the United States. For each token project, the region with the highest attention (SVI=100) represents the project's relative popularity in that region. 2. Initial Coin Offering (ICO) An ICO is a way for blockchain startups to raise funds. Unlike an IPO, these tokens do not represent equity, but usually represent some kind of utility within the project's ecosystem. (1) Sample screening: The data is integrated from ICOBench.io, and projects that do not meet the soft cap and cannot be participated in by US investors are excluded. (2) Data volume: 937 completed ICOs from January 2016 to December 2018 are finally selected. (3) Contributor identification: Wallet addresses are obtained through white papers, and the number of unique wallet addresses is tracked using Etherscan.io to infer the actual number of contributors. 3. Non-fungible tokens (NFTs) NFTs represent ownership of unique items (such as works of art) on the blockchain. (1) Sample screening: The data comes from the largest trading platform, OpenSea. Since Google Trends often displays 0 for low transaction volume entries, this article focuses on the top 100 NFT series by transaction volume between 2017 and 2022. (2) Screening criteria: Items with more than 10,000 units or an average minting price of 0 were excluded. The final sample included 46 NFT series. 4. Regional demographic characteristics This paper uses per capita lottery sales as a proxy variable for regional gambling propensity. Data were manually collected from state gambling authorities and aggregated at the DMA level. To avoid "forward-looking bias", all demographic data were set at the 2015 baseline to capture static cross-sectional differences. 5. Consumer credit characteristics Default data were obtained from Equifax (overdue for more than 90 days is considered a default). This paper calculates the default rate in the DMA-year-month dimension and divides the population into subprime (<620) and non-subprime (≥620) groups based on credit scores for comparative study. B. Descriptive Statistics 1. Regional Characteristics: In 197 DMAs with lottery data, the average annual lottery expenditure per adult is $199, but this varies greatly by region (ranging from less than $1 to over $800). 2. ICO Characteristics: The average amount raised by an ICO is $26.3 million (approximately 40% of the hard cap). 36% of projects require KYC (Know Your Customer) verification, and 57% of projects have their code publicly available on GitHub. 3. NFT Characteristics: The median number of NFTs issued in the sample is approximately 9,200. The vast majority (approximately 90%) are active on Twitter and Discord, and 85% of projects advertise the inclusion of "rare items."

III. Regional Gambling Tendencies and Retail Investor Crypto Attention

This study examines how regional gambling tendencies affect the attention received by crypto tokens by estimating the following general regression model:

Where SVL represents the attention received by a specified market region (DMA) d for an ICO or NFT series i during the issuance period.

 IV. Factors Driving Gambling-Style Token Attention

In this section, we explore the factors that moderate retail investors' gambling-style attention to crypto tokens, including the characteristics of the tokens themselves and changes in the external gambling environment.

A. Token Characteristic Analysis

This paper examines specific token attributes that may induce gambling psychology in retail investors.
1. Low Price Characteristic (Lottery-like Attribute): According to existing literature (Kumar (2009)), low price is a core characteristic of lottery-like stocks. Empirical studies have found that ICO projects with lower opening prices on the first day receive significantly more attention from regions with high gambling propensity than high-priced projects.

Interaction coefficients show that low-priced tokens received an additional 5% increase in attention in these regions. 2. KYC Protocols and Risk Preference: Price manipulation tactics such as "pump and depressurize" (P&D) schemes are common in the crypto market, and these projects typically have weak KYC (Know Your Customer) vetting. Research found that ICOs lacking KYC protocols attract significantly more attention from retail investors in high-gambling-prone regions, suggesting that these investors are more inclined to participate in high-risk, unregulated projects. 3. Market Bubble/Boom Effect: This paper defines the period from the second half of 2017 to the beginning of 2018 as the "boom period" of the ICO market and the price surge in the NFT market between 2021 and 2022 as the "boom period." Regression results show that token projects launched during these two periods received significantly more attention from high-gambling-prone regions than during non-bubble periods. For NFTs, high-gambling-prone regions showed approximately 23% more attention to tokens during the bubble period than during the non-bubble period.

B. The Impact of Sports Gambling Legalization

To further confirm that crypto attention is driven by gambling preferences, this paper uses the phased legalization of sports gambling in various US states as a natural experiment. If crypto tokens are seen as alternatives to gambling, then attention to tokens should decrease when legal gambling channels emerge. This paper estimates the following regression model:

Where, PostSG is a dummy variable that takes a value of 1 when the state where DMA d is located has legalized sports gambling and the ICO occurs after the legalization date.

Main Conclusions:

1. Significant Substitution Effect: Empirical results show that after the legalization of sports betting, the attention paid to ICOs in relevant regions decreased significantly.
2. Stronger Response in Regions with High Gambling Propensity: After introducing the interaction term between "legalization of gambling" and "regional per capita lottery sales," a significant negative correlation was found. This indicates that in regions with a pre-existing high gambling propensity, the "crowding-out effect" of opening up sports betting on cryptocurrency attention is most pronounced.
3. Conclusion Summary: This finding strongly demonstrates that retail investors view cryptocurrency as a substitute for traditional gambling products. When residents have legal channels for sports betting to satisfy their gambling appetites, their attention to the cryptocurrency market decreases accordingly.

V. Retail Investor Crypto Attention and Consequences of Consumer Credit

Existing research (Barber and Odean (2000); Barber et al. (2022)) shows that retail investors often underperform in traditional stock markets. If they perform similarly poorly in the crypto market, they may fall into financial distress. Therefore, this section examines the association between retail investor crypto attention and subsequent consumer credit consequences, and investigates how this association changes with consumer credit constraints. This paper measures credit constraints using credit scores and divides them into subprime borrowers (score < 620) and non-subprime borrowers (≥ 620). Since the ICO sample is more comprehensive than the NFT sample, this section focuses on the relationship between retail investor attention to ICOs and consumer default rates.

Existing research (Barber and Odean (2000); Barber et al. (2022)) shows that retail investors often underperform in traditional stock markets. If they also underperform in the crypto market, they may fall into financial distress. Therefore, this section examines the association between retail investor crypto attention and subsequent consumer credit consequences, and investigates how this association changes with consumer credit constraints.

This paper estimates the following regression model across a specified market area (DMA) – credit segment – ​​year – month dimension: [Image of regression model] The dependent variable is the change in default rate between the current month t and the next 6 months (t+6). HighSVI is a dummy variable representing the top third of monthly attention. Key Conclusions: 1. Association between Crypto Craze and Default Rate: The study found a significantly positive interaction term between per capita lottery sales (gambling tendency) and ICO attention indicators. This indicates that in regions with high game-theoretic tendencies and high ICO attention, subsequent consumer credit default rates show a significant increase. 2. Vulnerability of Subprime Borrowers: Further analysis shows that the surge in default rates was entirely driven by subprime borrowers. In regions with both high game-theoretic tendencies and high attention, the default rate for subprime borrowers increased by an average of approximately 2.3% over six months. In contrast, the default situation for non-subprime borrowers (those with better financial conditions) did not change significantly. 3. Lead-Lag Relationship and Preceding Trend Test: To rule out the possibility that default behavior itself caused the increase in attention, this paper conducted a preceding trend analysis on the changes in default rates. The conclusion shows that in the period before the surge in attention (t-6 to t), there were no significant differences in default rates across regions (no preceding trend); in the period after the surge in attention (t+1 to t+6), the subprime default rate in regions with high game-theoretic tendencies began to climb significantly. This lead-lag relationship suggests that the hype surrounding the crypto market foreshadowed the subsequent credit deterioration, not the other way around. **Summary:** This chapter demonstrates that game-driven crypto asset investment tendencies have negative economic consequences for vulnerable financial groups. For subprime borrowers already facing financial constraints, participation in such high-risk, lottery-like crypto investments often carries the risk of subsequent real financial default. **VI. Conclusion** This paper delves into the fundamental drivers of retail investor participation in the cryptocurrency market, finding that gambling preferences are the core factor explaining this phenomenon. By analyzing Google Trends search data, this paper confirms that in regions with higher per capita lottery sales and a stronger speculative atmosphere, retail investors show significantly higher interest in Initial Coin Offerings (ICOs) and Non-Fungible Tokens (NFTs) projects compared to other regions. This interest is not inflated; it is highly synchronized with the download and use of crypto wallets and directly and positively impacts the amount of funding raised and the number of participants in the primary market. Further moderating effect analysis shows that this gambling-driven investment motive is particularly strong during market "bubbles" and when tokens possess "lottery-like characteristics" (such as extremely low unit prices, lack of identity verification protocols/KYC, and susceptibility to price manipulation). The study also found, through a natural experiment using the legalization of sports betting in various US states, that when legal betting channels appear, the previously active interest in crypto tokens significantly decreases, strongly demonstrating that retail investors view crypto tokens as alternatives to traditional gambling products. Most importantly, this speculative behavior based on gambling preferences poses a substantial threat to individual and societal financial health. Using micro-data from Equifax Credit Bureau, the study found that a surge in crypto interest in regions with high gambling propensity often foreshadows a rise in consumer default rates in the following months, and this credit deterioration is concentrated entirely among the subprime borrowers with the weakest financial resilience. This finding shatters the simplistic illusion that "crypto assets are inclusive financial instruments," revealing their potential predatory nature as speculative tools that plunder the wealth of the lower classes. In conclusion, this paper provides important academic evidence for global regulators: crypto assets are largely used by retail investors as a new form of gambling; therefore, regulation of these assets should not be limited to financial risks but should also consider public health and consumer protection, establishing stricter disclosure standards and entry thresholds.

data.title: Pakistan Taps Trump-Linked World Liberty Financial to Test Stablecoin Payments
data.description:
Pakistan Tests Stablecoins as Part of State-Backed Payments Push

Pakistan has just signed an agreement to explore the use of a dollar-pegged stablecoin, marking the country's growing interest in blockchain-based settlement systems.

According to a Reuters report, the agreement involves SC Financial Technologies, a sister company of World Liberty Financial, the crypto venture associated with the family of U.S. President Donald Trump.

Today, World Liberty Financial signed an MoU with the Ministry of Finance to explore innovation in digital finance, particularly the use of stablecoins for cross-border transactions, signalling growing global interest in Pakistan as a key market for digital assets. pic.twitter.com/rYzbfHYysd

— Pakistan Virtual Assets Regulatory Authority (@PakistanVARA) January 14, 2026

The partnership would give Pakistan assess of World Liberty’s USD1 stablecoin which the country would utilise for cross-border transactions, marking one of the earliest known cases of a sovereign state formally evaluating a privately issued stablecoin for payments.

Under the reported arrangement, World Liberty Financial is expected to work with the State Bank of Pakistan to integrate USD1 into the country’s emerging digital payments architecture. The stablecoin would operate alongside existing systems, potentially supporting regulated use cases such as remittances, corporate settlements, and international transfers.

While the full terms of the agreement have not been disclosed, Pakistan is expected to formally announce the partnership during a visit to Islamabad by World Liberty chief executive Zach Witkoff. Public information on SC Financial Technologies remains limited, underscoring the early-stage and exploratory nature of the initiative.

The move reflects a broader shift among governments toward testing blockchain-based payment instruments not as speculative assets, but as infrastructure components capable of reducing settlement times and costs in cross-border finance.

USD1 adoption highlights institutional stablecoin momentum

USD1, the dollar-pegged stablecoin issued by World Liberty Financial, currently carries a market capitalization of approximately $3.4 billion, according to CoinMarketCap data. The token has already been used in large-scale transactions, including a reported $2 billion equity deal involving Binance and Abu Dhabi–based state investment firm MGX.

Pakistan Explores USD1 Stablecoin for Cross-Border Payments

Pakistan is reportedly exploring the use of USD1, a dollar-backed stablecoin issued by World Liberty Financial, a crypto venture linked to the Trump family, for cross-border payments.

The stablecoin could be integrated… pic.twitter.com/wexwugarG1

— ME Group (@MetaEraHK) January 14, 2026

Earlier this week, World Liberty expanded its ecosystem with the launch of World Liberty Markets, an onchain lending and borrowing platform built around USD1 and its WLFI governance token. The platform allows users to post crypto assets such as Ether, tokenized Bitcoin, and other stablecoins as collateral, reinforcing the project’s focus on institutional and capital markets use cases.

Pakistan positions itself as a testing ground for digital finance

Pakistan’s engagement with stablecoins fits into a broader national push to modernize its financial infrastructure and position itself as a regional crypto hub. Over the past year, authorities have moved to formalize the sector by establishing a dedicated virtual assets regulator, allowing major exchanges to operate domestically, and exploring initiatives such as Bitcoin reserves and real-world asset tokenization.

About yesterday at binance meetup event in islamabad,Pakistan binance is doing such a wonderful work to spread awarness of crypto in Pakistan looking forward to have it in my home city chakwal,Pakistan lets keep building guys @binance@cz_binance@UmairNauman_pic.twitter.com/k1AJPcvc7R

— Mohammadrafay (@Mohamma95814515) January 12, 2026

Officials have increasingly framed blockchain technology as a tool to address inefficiencies in cross-border payments and remittances, a critical component of Pakistan’s economy. Against this backdrop, testing a regulated stablecoin model offers policymakers a way to experiment with digital settlement rails without committing to a full central bank digital currency.

If implemented, the USD1 pilot could provide insight into how privately issued stablecoins might coexist with state oversight, offering a glimpse into how emerging markets are shaping the next phase of digital payments infrastructure.

data.title: Revolut Stablecoin Payments Surge 156% in 2025 Reaching $10.5 Billion as Users Embrace Everyday Transfers
data.description:
Revolut Sees Stablecoin Payments Soar to $10.5 Billion as Users Embrace Everyday Digital Dollars

Stablecoins are becoming a routine part of payments on Revolut, with transaction volumes estimated to have jumped 156% year over year to around $10.5 billion in 2025.

The surge highlights growing user adoption of digital dollars for everyday transfers, not just large-scale transactions.

Stablecoins Gaining Traction Among Revolut Users

While Revolut has not published official figures for 2025, crypto researcher Alex Obchakevich noted that stablecoins nearly doubled as a share of the platform’s total payments compared with 2024.

Citing data from Dune Analytics, he said,

“Despite the small absolute share, the dynamics are impressive.”

Obchakevich also highlighted that transfers ranging from $100 to $500 accounted for 30% to 40% of stablecoin activity, indicating users are increasingly turning to digital dollars for routine payments rather than only for large transfers.

Stablecoins on @Revolut are showing exponential growth. The volume of transactions with stablecoins is growing four times faster at 156% compared to the total payment volume of 38.5%.

This indicates the active implementation of crypto solutions on @Revolut. Over the year, the… https://t.co/1XBP5K07J5pic.twitter.com/TiO1JwowbE

— Alex (@obchakevich_) January 12, 2026

Which Stablecoins and Networks Lead the Growth

Ethereum dominates stablecoin flows on Revolut, representing more than two-thirds of transaction volume, followed by Tron at 22.8%.

The platform also supports Polygon, Solana, Arbitrum and Optimism, giving users flexibility in network selection based on speed and cost preferences.

Revolut’s push into stablecoins intensified in October 2025, when it introduced a feature allowing customers to exchange US dollars for USDC or USDT at a 1:1 rate, with no commissions or hidden fees.

Revolut is embracing stablecoins. Guarantees 1:1 USDT, USDC conversion on 6+ supported chains. pic.twitter.com/tHVxhFTyCI

— Max Karpis (@maxkarpis) December 19, 2025

The move simplified onchain transfers and reduced friction for users sending funds globally.

Global Stablecoin Market Expanding Rapidly

The surge on Revolut reflects wider growth in stablecoin adoption.

Bloomberg Intelligence projects that stablecoin payment flows could reach $56.6 trillion by 2030, growing at an 81% compound annual rate, driven by increasing retail use and institutional integration.

LATEST: 📊 Stablecoin payment flows could reach $56.6 trillion by 2030, up from $2.9 trillion in 2025, driven by institutional adoption and emerging market usage, according to Bloomberg Intelligence. pic.twitter.com/QjewfgMBnh

— CoinMarketCap (@CoinMarketCap) January 9, 2026

Overall, the global stablecoin market was valued at $312 billion in 2025, with the US Treasury projecting it could reach $2 trillion by 2028.

USDC led the market by transaction volume, processing $18.3 trillion, while Tether handled $13.3 trillion, despite remaining the largest stablecoin by market capitalization at $187 billion.

Analysts link the surge partly to the GENIUS Act, passed in July 2025, which provided clear legal guidance for payment stablecoins.

Are Everyday Payments Driving Stablecoin Adoption

The pattern of medium-sized transactions on Revolut indicates that stablecoins are moving beyond speculative use and into everyday financial activity.

Obchakevich noted:

“This indicates that Revolut users actively use stablecoins for everyday medium-sized payments, not just for large transfers.”

Other financial institutions are also exploring stablecoin-based solutions.

Western Union plans to roll out a Solana-based stablecoin settlement system in 2026, while MoneyGram and Zelle are introducing similar systems for faster cross-border payments.

"For 175 years, we've been connecting people, moving $150 billion a year. Digital assets is the next evolution.

We looked at alternatives, and came to the conclusion that Solana was the right choice."

- Devin McGranahan, CEO, Western Union pic.twitter.com/8ni2b47ktk

— Solana (@solana) October 28, 2025

How Revolut Supports This Growth

Revolut’s investment in blockchain infrastructure, including real-time transaction monitoring and compliance systems, has facilitated this expansion.

The platform ensures adherence to anti-money laundering requirements and screens transactions against global sanctions lists, enabling users to transact securely across multiple networks.

The combination of accessible tools, regulatory clarity, and practical use cases is driving stablecoins from niche interest to everyday payments on Revolut, signalling a shift in how digital currencies integrate into mainstream financial services.

data.title: We used a large model to "predict" the future of tokenization/Web3 in 2026.
data.description:
Given the same data, do you trust human interpretation or AI "fortune-telling"?

It sounds like a cyberpunk joke, but in today's market, where opinions are plentiful and highly emotional, what's scarce is precisely that unvarnished, genuine sample information.

After all, position sizing can really make people lie.

At the turn of the year, **in order to get as close as possible to the truth about tokenization/Web3 at the turning point of 2025-2026, we conducted an anonymous sampling of frontline Builders at Maiton MSX, focusing on three main questions:** What are your keywords for US stocks/tokenization in 2025? What are your actual holdings in 2025 (not limited to US stocks/Web3)? What sectors are you most willing to allocate to in 2026 (not limited to US stocks/Web3)? After some "coercion and enticement," a reasonably satisfactory sample size was collected and compressed into three word clouds almost entirely free of subjective judgment. Precisely because human interpretation inevitably carries bias, we decided to invite the "pure" ChatGPT, Gemini, and DeepSeek, under completely consistent prompt constraints, to "divine" these three word clouds. Perhaps AI really does understand this generation of Builders better than we do, and can better piece together the fate of US stocks, Web3, and tokenization in 2026 from these fragments. I. What are your keywords for US stocks/tokenization in 2025?

Prompt: You will see a set of keyword samples from frontline practitioners in the US stock tokenization/Web3 industry. These keywords are used to answer the same question: "What is your vision for the US stock/tokenization sector in 2025?". Do not judge the correctness of the statements, nor introduce any external market information. Based solely on these keywords, interpret them from the perspective of data structure and language characteristics, focusing on answering:

Which keywords have the highest/lowest frequency/are relatively abrupt, and what structural state might they reflect? Which keywords or semantic directions show obvious consensus characteristics? Are there keyword combinations with similar meanings, opposite directions, or strong tensions? What stage of development does this sector collectively suggest? If you only rely on these keywords, without introducing any external information, how would you understand this sector in 2025 and 2026?

Please maintain a descriptive and restrained analytical tone, avoiding investment advice or definitive judgments. Replies should not exceed 1000 words. Editor's Note: Replies have been slightly edited and polished to enhance readability, but the overall direction and approach have not been significantly adjusted (the same applies below).

ChatGPT: The most frequent keywords are concentrated on "acceleration" and "institutionalization," suggesting that the track is undergoing a process of significant change in pace but has not yet stabilized; the mid-frequency keywords include "AI," "compliance," and "differentiation," reflecting a backgrounded and normalized existence; low-frequency or isolated words such as "weightlessness," "unknown," "strategic battleground," and "starting point" carry strong subjective feelings or emotional metaphors, which may reflect individuals' discomfort, sense of competition, or uncertainty about structural changes. The overall corpus exhibits linguistic characteristics of the "early-middle stage—structural building phase," with more high-frequency verbs than nouns, indicating that the focus of discussion remains on process and change rather than stable forms; multiple keywords point to rules, verification, and processes, rather than specific products or models; emotional vocabulary is present but not dominant, indicating that uncertainty is perceived but not yet out of control. Based solely on these keywords, 2025 can be understood as a transitional year of acceleration but not yet finalization, where consensus is gathering but simultaneously diverging. Gemini: High-frequency words such as "acceleration" and "institutionalization" indicate a strong consensus on growth following the intervention of professional forces; mid-frequency words such as "AI," "compliance," and "differentiation" outline the uneven performance of technology, regulatory frameworks, and the market; and relatively abrupt low-frequency words such as "weightlessness," "process-oriented," and "asset verification" reflect underlying anxieties beneath the surface of prosperity and a shift from narrative to a micro-perspective of implementation. Overall, the data presents a state of "high momentum, low center of gravity." The coexistence of "institutionalization" and "compliance" suggests that this acceleration is not blind expansion but a structural shift constrained by rules, forming a certain tone for the industry. Based on the overall characteristics of the keywords, this sector is currently in a "transitional explosive phase," exhibiting both the chaos (lack of direction, uncertainty) of the initial stage and the rigor (compliance, process-oriented) of the eve of maturity. The market is no longer monolithic but shows obvious "differentiation," indicating that tokenization is no longer an isolated asset experiment but is beginning to be deeply coupled with mainstream finance and cutting-edge technologies. Overall, from 2025 to 2026, this will be a period of frantic progress amidst anxiety, reshaping within the rules, and a highly professional entry point heavily reliant on technological validation. DeepSeek: "Acceleration" is the absolute core, with "institutionalization," "differentiation," "compliance," and "AI" forming the second tier. Most words appear only once, exhibiting a discrete long-tail characteristic. For example, "weightlessness" seems rather abrupt in a generally dynamic and positive context (such as acceleration, opening up, breaking boundaries), possibly implying a temporary loss of the traditional frame of reference amidst rapid change. "Institutionalization" and "compliance" are closely related, both pointing to the trend of deep involvement of traditional financial system rules and participants. "Differentiation" suggests the potential separation of levels or paths within the market or sector. The dynamic nature of sector development (acceleration, variability, volatility) and the institutionalization process (institutionalization, compliance, process-oriented, structured) constitute the two strongest sets of consensus semantic directions. Both approaches run parallel, depicting a field seeking to establish rules and structures amidst rapid change. II. Your Actual Holdings in 2025 (Not Limited to US Stocks/Web3) Prompt: You will see a set of keyword samples from frontline practitioners in US stock tokenization/Web3, used to answer the same question: "Your actual holdings in 2025 (Not Limited to US Stocks/Web3)". Do not judge right or wrong, nor introduce any external market information; only conduct logical analysis based on the composition of the samples, interpreting from a data structure perspective: Classify the involved targets/sectors by asset type; Are there core assets that are repeatedly allocated? Is the overall holding more concentrated or diversified, revealing what kind of risk tolerance? What behavioral characteristics can be observed from these actual holdings? Please maintain a descriptive and restrained analytical tone, avoiding investment advice or definitive judgments. Replies should not exceed 1000 characters. [Image of a screenshot: https://img.foresightnews.pro/202601/32171-1768313634615.jpg?x-oss-process=style/scale70] ChatGPT: From the sample structure, holdings can be clearly divided into several asset clusters.

  • Crypto-native assets:High-frequency terms include BTC, ETH, SOL, BNB, USDT, USDC), while secondary/edge terms include XRP, DOGE, ASTER, SUPRA, XTTA; this category has a clear advantage in terms of quantity and repetition, and includes both mainstream assets and exploratory allocations;

  • US stock technology and finance related:Including technology/platform types—GOOGL, AAPL, AMAZN, NVDA, TSLA, PLTR, INTC, TSM, MU, WDC; and finance/trading related—COIN, HOOD; showing a preference for infrastructure, platform, and technological capabilities;

data.title: Polygon Moves Into Regulated US Payments with Coinme’s Fiat Services and Sequence’s On-Chain Infrastructure
data.description:
Polygon Gains Regulatory Approval to Operate as US Payments Platform After Coinme and Sequence Acquisitions

Polygon Labs is moving closer to becoming a fully regulated payments platform in the United States following its acquisition of Seattle-based crypto payments firm Coinme and Toronto-based crypto infrastructure company Sequence.

The deals give Polygon access to regulated money movement across 48 U.S. states, as well as fiat on- and off-ramps, and 50,000 physical fiat-to-crypto locations nationwide.

BREAKING: Polygon to become U.S. regulated payments platform

We’re acquiring Coinme and Sequence to move all money onchain.
→ Regulated money movement in 48 states
→ Fiat on/off ramps
→ 50,000 fiat-to-crypto locations in the U.S.
→ Easy onboarding with wallet infra
→… pic.twitter.com/lwvLheEc3P

— Polygon | POL (@0xPolygon) January 13, 2026

Coinme, established in 2014, allows users to convert cash into cryptocurrency and brings over a million existing users and its US-licenced infrastructure to Polygon.

Sequence, originally founded as Horizon in 2017, provides modular wallet infrastructure, one-click crypto transactions, versatile gas payments, and cross-chain functionality.

How Will Polygon Integrate Coinme and Sequence

Polygon Foundation CEO Sandeep Nailwal wrote on X,

“Today, we’re reaching a pivotal milestone for the Polygon Open Money Stack by acquiring Coinme and Sequence.

Polygon’s blockchain rails have been ready for years. But we were missing the last mile pieces required to operate compliant, end-to-end payments at scale.

Regulated fiat access in the U.S. across 48 states, compliant onboarding infra, and seamless crosschain payments through intents.”

He added,

“Bringing Coinme and Sequence into Polygon completes the vertically integrated stack offering…This is how we win in payments.”

The integration will combine Coinme’s regulated fiat services with Sequence’s on-chain infrastructure to simplify crypto transactions for businesses and consumers.

Less than one week ago we shared our vision for the next evolution of Polygon, and for global finance as a whole.

Today, we’re reaching a pivotal milestone for the Polygon Open Money Stack by acquiring @Coinme and @0xsequence.

Polygon’s blockchain rails have been ready for…

— Sandeep | CEO, Polygon Foundation (※,※) (@sandeepnailwal) January 13, 2026

Investment and Market Positioning

Polygon Labs reportedly paid over $250 million USD ($347 million CAD) for both acquisitions, though the exact breakdown for each company was not disclosed.

CEO Marc Boiron clarified reports suggesting Coinme alone was valued between $100 million and $125 million were inaccurate.

Polygon’s Ethereum-based blockchain has processed $2.2 trillion USD in value and supports payments for major platforms including Stripe, Revolut, Flutterwave, and Polymarket.

Polygon has already established the standard for production-grade blockchain rails, facilitating $2.2 trillion in onchain value transfer. By combining forces with Sequence and Coinme, the Open Money Stack delivers the modular full-stack multi-chain platform that moves all money…

— Michael Sanders (@_michaelsanders) January 13, 2026

The company sees stablecoins as a central part of future payments infrastructure but acknowledges that connecting them to traditional financial systems has been challenging.

Coinme’s regulated fiat ramps and Sequence’s developer tools aim to bridge that gap.

Will Polygon Compete With Traditional Payments Networks

Marc Boiron told Reuters that the company’s immediate focus will be business-to-business payments, with consumer services expected to follow.

He suggested that Polygon’s approach will prioritise partnerships rather than direct competition with established players such as Visa and Mastercard.

He said,

“In five or 10 years, we will find out if cards are still going to be necessary. But for the time being, we can work together quite collaboratively and grow the pie.”

interesting to see and one of the reasons this became possible is because blue chip payment companies like stripe, revolut, mastercard, and monerium partnership validated polygon.

other businesses saw established names using Polygon and gain confidence, which led to more USDC… https://t.co/yRyHNkXMIx

— Hokage (@73lV_) December 30, 2025

Canadian Web3 Talent Draws Global Interest

Sequence’s acquisition is another example of Canadian Web3 firms being snapped up by foreign companies.

Similar moves include Ripple’s $275 million CAD purchase of Toronto-based Rail and Robinhood Markets’ planned $250 million CAD acquisition of WonderFi.

Sequence’s products will continue to operate independently, allowing customers to build on its platform without disruption.

Stablecoins Set to Drive Crypto Payments Growth

Polygon’s strategy leverages the rising interest in stablecoin payments, following regulatory developments such as the U.S. Genius Act.

📊 @0xPolygon just had its 11th consecutive week of growth for micropayments with 67.7M last week pic.twitter.com/6775MDrWFi

— Token Relations 📊 (@TokenRelations) January 12, 2026

Coinme’s fiat infrastructure and Sequence’s cross-chain capabilities provide the building blocks for a more seamless payments experience, aiming to make blockchain payments a practical option for both businesses and consumers.

The acquisitions also coincide with a strong performance for Polygon’s native POL token, which rose 53% over the past two weeks.

data.title: Gold has hit a new high. What are the ways for ordinary users to participate in the gold market?
data.description:
Author: Blockchain Knight

On January 13th, the U.S. Senate Banking Committee released the full text of the "Clarity Act for Digital Asset Markets," which is expected to be reviewed this week.

This 278-page draft abandons the token-by-token classification model and constructs a "channel system" for allocating jurisdiction based on the functional lifecycle of digital assets. Its core is to clarify the regulatory boundaries between the SEC and CFTC.

The market is optimistic about this, with Polymarket users believing there is an 80% chance the bill will be signed into law this year. However, senators only have a 48-hour window to propose amendments, making time tight.

The core of the bill is to build a regulatory bridge between the SEC and the CFTC: "auxiliary assets" that rely on the efforts of the issuers will be subject to SEC regulation, and issuers will be required to disclose detailed information such as audited financial statements, ownership, and token economics, in line with the standards for listed companies; after the control of the tokens is decentralized, they will be converted into "digital commodities" and the CFTC will regulate the trading venues and intermediaries. Key terms directly address industry pain points: First, an ETF fast track: after January 1, 2026, tokens that are the primary assets of listed ETPs will not be considered auxiliary assets, effectively opening a commodity market channel for mainstream cryptocurrencies such as BTC and ETH; Second, clear definition of Ethereum staking: staking rewards are defined as "uncompensated distribution," providing a safe channel for self-staking, self-custody staking, and liquidity staking, while staking on custodial exchanges remains strictly regulated; Third, demarcation of stablecoin yields: issuers are prohibited from paying yields for holding stablecoins, but are allowed to earn yields through DeFi lending and other methods, maintaining the economic viability of DeFi yields; Fourth, a DeFi safe harbor: interfaces that are not custodial, do not control private keys, and do not interfere with transactions are considered pure software, exempting broker registration, while centralized entities are subject to strict regulation. Bitwise believes it could drive the market to new highs, but legal experts are racing against time to fix flaws to meet the amendment deadline. Criticism focuses on privacy and decentralization, arguing that universal registration stifles anonymous participation, and government custodians are demanding a de facto ban on self-custody; the wording on stablecoin yields and the addition of a DeFi chapter have also sparked controversy. Although the bill has passed the House of Representatives, Senate negotiations remain focused on differences regarding banking priorities, and some industry insiders are pessimistic about its prospects for passage.

data.title: Is your cryptocurrency holding and trading safe? In-depth analysis of the criminal red lines of virtual currency.
data.description:
Recently, the Shanghai Second Intermediate People's Court, in conjunction with relevant units, held a seminar on crimes involving virtual currencies. Important consensus was reached regarding the legal characterization of virtual currency-related behaviors, with the conclusion that "individual holding and trading of virtual currencies is generally not considered illegal business operations" providing clear guidance for judicial practice and market behavior. However, the other side of this consensus is a more explicit "red line." The judiciary is not completely opening up virtual currency-related behaviors, but rather precisely targeting illegal and criminal activities that, under the guise of innovation, actually disrupt financial management order. So, where exactly is the boundary between individual investors and the market? Which seemingly ordinary behaviors actually harbor criminal risks? The Sa Jie team will delve into the key signals released by this seminar, clarifying the "safe zone" and the "red line" for you, helping you navigate the crypto world with stability and success.

I. Safe Zone: Why Doesn't "Individual Cryptocurrency Trading" Constitute the Crime of Illegal Business Operations?

The law targets "business operations," not "investment."

First, we need to understand the core elements of the "crime of illegal business operations" stipulated in Article 225 of my country's Criminal Law, which requires the simultaneous fulfillment of the following points:

  1. The behavior is "business-oriented." This refers to providing services to the general public continuously, openly, and on a large scale for profit; violating national regulations. This business activity clearly violates mandatory provisions of national laws and administrative regulations; disrupting market order. The behavior has reached a serious level, disrupting the normal market management order of the state. The case of Li, mentioned at the seminar, involved the following pattern: **using price differences between domestic and foreign platforms to buy and sell between personal accounts and profit from the difference.** Why doesn't this pattern of behavior constitute a crime? The court held that Li's behavior was **"personal arbitrage"**. His counterparties are other unspecified retail investors on the platform. He did not actively solicit or promise to the public to provide exchange or trading services, nor did he establish channels or collect fixed fees as an intermediary. This is essentially a **personal investment (speculation)** activity, lacking the public, continuous, and service-oriented characteristics of "business." The judiciary has drawn a clear line here: as an end user, your buying, holding, and selling (even frequently) for the purpose of increasing your own assets falls within the scope of **personal property disposal**, with extremely low criminal risk. This provides basic legal security for personal asset management strategies.

II. Red Lines: These Two Types of Behaviors are the Focus of Criminal Crackdown

However, the "safe zone" mentioned above does not mean that one can freely engage in any activity related to virtual currency. The seminar clearly drew two untouchable red lines, the essence of which is to **transform virtual currency into a tool for illegal financial activities**.

data.title: 45% of speculative positions were liquidated; Bitcoin is poised for a fresh start in 2026.
data.description:
Executive Summary

• Bitcoin enters 2026 after a decisive pullback and consolidation phase. On-chain metrics show easing profit-taking pressure and early signs of structural stabilization at the lower end of the current range.

• A large amount of "upper supply" remains concentrated at the upper part of the price range, limiting breakout attempts and highlighting the importance of recovering key recovery thresholds before a trend resumes.
• Corporate treasury demand continues to provide support below the price, but this demand is intermittent rather than a sustained structural buying. US spot ETF flows are beginning to re-emerge after outflows at the end of 2025.
• Futures open interest has stabilized and turned upward, reflecting an initial recovery in institutional participation.
The options market experienced its largest open interest reset on record (clearing over 45% of positions), removing structural hedging constraints and providing a clearer signal for new risk expressions. Implied volatility may have bottomed out, with moderate buying demand at the start of the year pushing up the volatility curve. Skewness continues to normalize, put premiums are compressing, call participation is increasing, and fund flows are shifting from defensive hedging to bullish participation. Traders' Gamma reversed to short in the $95K–$104K range, meaning that rising prices will mechanically push prices further up. Overall, the market is transitioning from defensive deleveraging to selective risk-taking, opening 2026 with a clearer structure and renewed expansion of options.

On-chain Insights

Easing Profit-Taking Pressure

• In the first week of 2026, Bitcoin broke through a long-term compression zone around $87k, rebounding approximately 8.5% to $94.4k. This rally was accompanied by a significant easing of profit-taking pressure.

• In late December 2025, realized profits (7-day moving average) dropped sharply to $183.8 million per day, well below the levels of over $1 billion per day observed for most of the fourth quarter.

• This slowdown in realized gains (especially among long-term holders) indicates that the selling pressure that previously suppressed the price has been exhausted.

• As selling pressure eased, the market stabilized and supported a new upward pulse. Therefore, the breakout in early January reflects that the market had effectively reset profit-taking pressure, allowing gold prices to rise. Facing Supply Above • As profit-taking eased, prices rose, but the current rally is pushing into a different supply system. The market has entered an area dominated by recent high-buyers, with a cost base densely distributed between $92.1k and $117.4k. • According to the realized price distribution of UTXO, this group accumulated a large amount of capital near the cycle high and held positions through a pullback. Now, with the price rebound, these investors have regained the opportunity to exit at breakeven, thus creating natural selling resistance. • For a sustained bull market to resume, the market needs time to digest the supply of trapped capital above this level.

Key Recovery Threshold

• During this transition period, the cost basis of short-term holders is a key navigational tool.

• The fragile balance in December formed near the lower bound of the model (-1 standard deviation). The subsequent rebound brought the price back to near the model's mean, $99.1k. The first meaningful confirmation of a market recovery will be a sustained recovery of the short-term holder cost base ($99.1k), which would signal a restoration of confidence among new market participants.

Profitability or Long-Term Pain

• If the above levels cannot be recovered, the structure will resemble the failed case in the first quarter of 2022, potentially leading to a deeper bearish extension.

• The short-term holder MVRV indicator has currently rebounded from a low of 0.79 to 0.95.

This means that investors are still in an average unrealized loss of about 5% in the near term. • If a decisive and sustained return to profitability (STH-MVRV > 1) cannot be achieved, the probability of a continuation of the bear market will increase.

Off-Chain Insights

Corporate Treasury Demand Retreat

• While corporate treasury cash flow is an important source of marginal demand, it exhibits irregular and highly event-driven characteristics. They tend to buy during local pullbacks rather than accumulate continuously. Currently, this demand acts more as a "buffer" below prices than a trend driver.

ETF Demand Re-emerges

• After experiencing net outflows at the end of 2025, US spot ETFs have recently seen a clear inflection point of positive inflows. Although the size has not reached its peak, the direction has turned to buying, indicating that institutional spot demand is once again becoming a constructive tailwind factor.

Futures Participation Rebuilding

• Futures open interest has stabilized and rebounded after a significant deleveraging (retreating from highs above $50B). This recovery in positions, coupled with price stabilization in the $80k-$90k range, indicates that traders are gradually rebuilding their risk exposure rather than aggressively chasing the rally. Current open interest is far below previous cyclical extremes, reducing the risk of immediate liquidation.

• The slight expansion of open interest indicates improved risk appetite and a rebuilding of derivatives buying, which will support near-term price discovery as liquidity conditions begin to normalize at the start of the new year. [Figure 7]

A Thorough Cleanup of Positions

• The end of 2025 marks the largest reset of Bitcoin options open interest on record. After expiring on December 26, open interest plummeted by more than 45%. • This reset cleared away the price "pegging" effect previously caused by traders holding large Gamma positions. The market is now no longer constrained by those hedging mechanisms, and the newly opened positions better reflect traders' true risk appetite for the future [Figure 8] Implied volatility bottoms out. • Option implied volatility hit a short-term low during the Christmas period. Subsequently, as buyer interest returned (especially to the bullish side), implied volatility began to rise moderately, but remained low overall (with maturities of 1 week to 6 months clustered between 42.6% and 45.4%). This suggests that the current rise in volatility is more a reflection of restored participation than a decisive repricing of risk.

Skewing Continues to Normalize

• Option skewness shows that put options are becoming less expensive relative to call options. Demand is shifting from simple downside protection to bullish participation. This is consistent with traders rebuilding their risk exposure after the year-end reset. Overall, market risk expression is becoming more balanced, increasingly reflecting openness to upward price action or increased volatility, rather than openness to tail risk pricing.

New Year Fund Flows Bullish

• Since the beginning of the year, options flows show an increase in the proportion of call option purchases (accounting for 30.8% of total activity). This demand has been met by volatility sellers. The proportion of downside protection activity has decreased, reflecting a reduction in market fear of tail risk.

Traders Turn Short on Gamma in Upper Range

• With the influx of bullish buying at the start of the new year, traders are primarily holding short Gamma positions between $95K and $104K.
• Within this range, price increases will force traders to buy spot or perpetual contracts to hedge, which will mechanically boost prices during the upward movement. This is a stark contrast to the bullish environment at the end of the year. The Gamma profile reflects the fulcrum of the market structure at the beginning of the year, formed by new upward demand.

95K Call Option Premium Reflects Confidence

• Since January 1st, buying of $95K call options has accelerated. Although recent buying volume has leveled off, there hasn't been a large-scale sell-off to close positions. This indicates that bullish participants are showing patience and are willing to hold positions rather than quickly taking profits.

Conclusion

Bitcoin enters the new year with most historical positions (spot, futures, and options) cleared out. The deleveraging event at the end of 2025 and the year-end settlement effectively reset the structural constraints of the market, leaving a cleaner and more transparent environment. Stabilized ETF flows, rebuilt futures participation, and a bullish turn in the options market (skewness normalization, volatility bottoming out, and dealers turning short with Gamma) all indicate that the market is shifting from defensive distribution behavior to selective risk-taking. While structural accumulation remains restrained, with the "obstacles cleared," Bitcoin will have an improved internal market structure and renewed expansive options at the start of 2026.

data.title: Meta Accidentally Leaks Emails And Usernames of 17.5 Million Users But Denies Any Wrongdoing
data.description:
Instagram Data Leak Claims Spark Confusion as Meta Denies Breach

Reports of a potential large-scale Instagram data leak triggered concern this week after cybersecurity firm Malwarebytes claimed that millions of user records were discovered for sale on underground forums. Meta, the parent company of Instagram, has rejected the allegations, stating that no breach occurred and that user data remains secure.

‼️17.5 Million Instagram Users' Data Leaked

The data includes phone numbers, emails, usernames, user IDs, and physical addresses.

This appears to be from the Instagram 2024 API breach, in which 489 million records were obtained. (See second screenshot) pic.twitter.com/xNKfavaFun

— International Cyber Digest (@IntCyberDigest) January 10, 2026

According to Malwarebytes, data tied to approximately 17.5 million Instagram users appeared on dark web marketplaces during routine monitoring. The cybersecurity firm said the exposed information includes usernames, email addresses, phone numbers, home addresses, and other personal details. Researchers suggested the dataset may be linked to an Instagram API exposure dating back to 2024.

Shortly after the report emerged, social media platforms were flooded with user complaints about repeated password reset emails that had not been requested. The timing of the messages intensified speculation that attackers were attempting to exploit leaked data or test which accounts remained active.

Meta disputed Malwarebytes’ findings and denied that Instagram systems had been compromised. In a public statement, the company said a technical flaw temporarily allowed external actors to trigger password reset emails for some users, but emphasized that the issue did not involve unauthorized access to accounts or internal databases.

🚨 ALERT: Instagram data breach exposes personal information of 17.5 million users.

Emails, phone numbers, addresses and usernames reportedly leaked and sold on the dark web, per Malwarebytes.

We need Blockchain based web3 social media and Online+ is the safe & future. $IONpic.twitter.com/8iEAsq9RPu

— Ethereum Gem Sniper (@ETHgemsniper) January 11, 2026

Meta claims that the problem has since been resolved and advised users to ignore the unexpected emails, reiterating that no evidence points to a data breach and that account security remains intact.

Experts warn leaked data can still pose risks

Despite Meta’s assurances, security experts caution that the exposure of personal information — even without direct account access — can still enable cybercrime. Researchers note that leaked contact details are frequently used in phishing campaigns, impersonation attempts, and broader identity fraud schemes.

With access to real usernames, email addresses, or phone numbers, attackers can craft convincing messages designed to trick users into revealing login credentials or verification codes. In some cases, bad actors may attempt password recovery on other platforms linked to the same contact information or impersonate victims to target friends and followers.

Experts also warn that sudden waves of password reset emails can signal attackers probing databases to identify valid or vulnerable accounts. Even in the absence of a confirmed breach, the availability of personal data increases the likelihood of successful social engineering attacks elsewhere.

User security remains in focus amid ongoing scrutiny

Security professionals continue to urge Instagram users to take precautionary steps, including enabling two-factor authentication, updating old or reused passwords, and treating unexpected messages with skepticism. Emails or direct messages requesting immediate action or personal information are commonly used in phishing campaigns and should be handled with care.

16 billion passwords leaked in a massive data breach

What you need to know to protect your Facebook, Instagram, Gmail and other accounts 🔗https://t.co/2b9tQWPDgw

— The Times Of India (@timesofindia) June 20, 2025

The incident adds to a growing history of scrutiny surrounding Instagram’s data practices. In late 2024, reports surfaced claiming that nearly 489 million Instagram records had appeared on the dark web — allegations Meta also denied at the time.

While the company maintains that user data remains protected, the conflicting narratives surrounding the latest claims highlight persistent concerns over transparency, data protection, and trust as cyber threats continue to evolve.
