data.description:
Deng Tong, Jinse Finance

Overnight, the cryptocurrency market seems to have returned to a bull market, with BTC and ETH breaking through $96,000 and $3,300 respectively. In addition, precious metals, US stocks, and A-shares continue their previous bull market. What is driving the investment market higher? How will the cryptocurrency market develop in the future?

I. Precious Metals, Stock Market, and Cryptocurrency Market Outlook

1. Gold

On January 14th, spot gold rose more than $50 intraday, hitting a record high of $4,639.11 per ounce, a gain of 1.12%. On January 13th, State Street Investment Management strategists pointed out in a report that based on recent price momentum and geopolitical dynamics, the probability of spot gold breaking through $5,000 per ounce this year is now over 30%. In 2026, gold will be in a favorable position, supported by factors including surging global debt burdens, the direction of Federal Reserve policy, and potential volatility shocks.

Strategists believe that the correlation between US stocks and bonds is likely to remain positive in 2026, which increases the space for gold allocation in portfolios as investors may seek alternatives to liquidity. Meanwhile, central bank gold purchases are expected to support physical demand, providing a stable anchor for the precious metals market. 2. Silver On January 14th, some trading platforms showed that spot silver broke through the $90/ounce mark for the first time, setting a new record high, with a year-to-date increase of nearly 26%. Silver's market capitalization exceeded $5 trillion, ranking as the world's second-largest asset, with spot gold ranking first and Nvidia ranking third. 3. US Stocks US stocks closed at a record high on January 12th. According to Dow Jones market data, the S&P 500 and Dow Jones Industrial Average both rose 0.2%, closing at record highs of 6977.32 points and 49590.20 points, respectively. The tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite rose 0.3%, just 0.9% below its record closing close at the end of October last year. Trump tweeted: "Under my leadership, the American economy is booming! A manufacturing resurgence and a surge in household income are driving GDP growth to levels not seen in decades. We are experiencing a deflationary economic boom. Private sector growth is exceeding 5%, driven by unprecedented, perhaps historic, corporate investment, and inflation looks pretty good. All smart money knows that the hottest economy in the world is the United States." Andrew Slimmon, senior portfolio manager for U.S. equities at Morgan Stanley Investment Management, said that the optimism surrounding further interest rate cuts under the Trump-appointed Federal Reserve chairman has been a key theme for investors this year, with many expecting looser monetary policy to support the U.S. economy. Slimmon stated that he believes the U.S. stock market rally is fundamentally driven and will be a healthy component of a sustained bull market not only last year but also through 2026—even if it appears to be in its later stages. 4. A-Shares In the first week of trading in 2026, the A-share market continued its upward trend, with the Shanghai Composite Index, Shenzhen Component Index, and ChiNext Index rising by 3.82%, 4.40%, and 3.89% respectively. Regarding the reasons for the continuous rise of the A-share market since the beginning of 2026, Fang Lei, Deputy General Manager and Chief Strategist of Xing Shi Investment, believes that the market's strong performance is mainly due to the accumulation of positive factors, high investor sentiment, and sensitivity to positive factors. Liquidity factors and policy expectations have also contributed to the stock market's performance, and this round of market gains is not driven by a single factor. In addition, the continued appreciation of the RMB at the end of the year and the beginning of the new year, with the USD/CNH exchange rate breaking through the key level of 7, has also supported the rise in market sentiment. 5. Crypto Market This morning, BTC broke through $96,000, and as of press time, it was trading at $95,020, a 24-hour increase of 3.6%. ETH broke through $3350, reaching $3329.89 as of press time, a 24-hour increase of 6.3%. XRP rose 4.3% in 24 hours; BNB rose 3.6%; SOL rose 3.7%; DOGE rose 6.5%; and ADA rose 6.5%. The latest market analysis from Xinhuo Research Institute shows that the directional breakout in the crypto market has arrived as expected, fully validating the previous accurate prediction of market "bottoming out and accumulating strength." The industry is ushering in a new round of accelerated upward movement. Ding Yuan, Dean of Xinhuo Research Institute, pointed out that this market breakout is a precise realization of the institute's previous judgment. Last week, Bitcoin's 30-day implied volatility dropped to a yearly low of 40%, and this "calm before the storm" was precisely the turning point signal that Xinhuo Research Institute highlighted. With the surge in prices early this morning, Bitcoin broke through the $96,000 mark, with a 24-hour increase of 4.4%; Ethereum simultaneously climbed to $3,300, with a 24-hour increase of 7.4%, marking the official end of the market's consolidation pattern and the establishment of a fully established upward channel. Ding Yuan believes that the logic behind this round of gains is solid: the current macroeconomic environment remains stable in January and February, coupled with multiple major positive factors such as the new $15 billion fund from a16z heavily investing in the crypto sector and South Korea's complete lifting of the ban on crypto investment, market liquidity is returning at an unexpected speed. The research institute maintains its core judgment—Bitcoin will challenge the $100,000 mark in the short term, and is firmly optimistic about Ethereum's "revenge" rebound after its oversold condition.

II. What is driving the soaring prices of various investment products?

1. Interest rate cut expectations

The US Consumer Price Index rose 2.7% year-on-year in December, in line with expectations and the previous value of 2.7%.

Following the release of the CPI data, the probability of a Federal Reserve rate cut in April rose to 42%, up from 38% before the data release. Trump stated: "When the market is rising, the Fed should lower interest rates. In the past, interest rates have fallen when the economy was doing well. There's a real deadlock in the Fed. The Fed chairman has killed every economic rebound. Powell is 'going away soon.' 'The US (December) inflation data is out, very low! This means Jerome 'Too Late' Powell should cut rates significantly! If he doesn't, he'll just continue to say 'Too Late'! Besides, the US economic growth data is also very good. Thank you, Mr. Tariffs!'" Rick Rieder, BlackRock's global chief fixed income investment officer and a potential successor to Fed Chairman Powell, is reportedly scheduled to meet with President Trump on Thursday. At this time, Rieder reiterated his support for lowering the US benchmark interest rate to 3%, a level that would be the lowest in more than three years. In a CNBC interview aired Monday, Riddell stated that he has repeatedly expressed his desire for interest rates to fall to 3% for months. He reiterated his support for this move on Monday, which would lower borrowing costs by at least 50 basis points (0.5 percentage points) from current levels. The current target range for the federal funds rate is 3.5%–3.75% after Federal Reserve officials cut rates by 25 basis points in December. “I think the Fed does have some policy room,” Riddell said. “My stance has been very clear over the past few months. The Fed must lower rates, and I don’t think it needs to be lowered too much, eventually to 3%—that level is closer to the neutral rate.” The neutral rate is a theoretical level of borrowing costs that is neither stimulative nor restrictive, and can maintain the smooth operation of the US economy. The 2026 US midterm elections are scheduled for November 3, when all 435 seats in the House of Representatives, 35 seats in the Senate, and some governor and local official seats will be up for election. The midterm elections significantly influence policy expectations, liquidity conditions, and market risk appetite, and are a key driver of the current positive investment market. However, Trump's situation is clearly not optimistic. Trump has pleaded with Republicans to reverse their political decline before the November midterm elections, warning that he would face a third impeachment if the Democrats regain control of Congress. "You have to win the midterm elections. They will find a reason to impeach me. I will be impeached," Trump predicted, forecasting an "epic" Republican victory that would reverse the previous trend. However, polls show public dissatisfaction with his leadership and the economy, a somber sign for the Republicans' ability to maintain control of Congress. Under electoral pressure, the ruling party typically favors loose monetary policy to secure votes. In 2026, the Trump administration, to solidify its Republican majority in Congress, pushed for fiscal stimulus and unexpected interest rate cuts by the Federal Reserve, providing liquidity and profit support for assets such as stocks and gold. On January 8th, he was “instructing my representatives” to purchase $200 billion in mortgage-backed securities, claiming this would lower interest rates and monthly payments. The two government-backed mortgage lenders—Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac—are currently well-funded. “Right now, I’m paying particular attention to the housing market,” Trump said, adding that this move is the latest effort to lower housing costs ahead of the November midterm elections. Additionally, according to media reports, the White House is drafting an executive order aimed at broadly addressing Americans’ dissatisfaction with the cost of living, including pushing for allowing people to use retirement funds and college savings accounts to pay for down payments on homes. 3. The Crypto Markets Structure Act is Imminent For the crypto market, perhaps the biggest direct influencing factor recently is the impending release of the Crypto Markets Structure Act. On January 13th, according to crypto journalist Eleanor Terrett, an incomplete draft of the Senate Banking Committee's Market Structure Bill was revealed. This draft includes the following: Two minor ethics clauses falling under the jurisdiction of the Banking Committee—unlike most ethics-related provisions (which are typically drafted by other committees and will not appear in this draft or the version of the bill to be released within an hour). Notably, page 72 of the draft deals with felony convictions, and page 270 contains provisions related to insider trading. A compromise appears to have been reached between the DeFi and traditional finance (TradFi) sectors, embodied in Section 601 protecting software developers. According to industry sources close to the negotiations, the two sides finalized the agreement this week after tense closed-door meetings last week. A key concern for traditional finance practitioners was the potential for decentralized finance protocols to be used for regulatory arbitrage, a point particularly strongly voiced by securities industry associations such as the Securities Industry and Financial Markets Association (SIFMA). The U.S. Senate Agriculture Committee plans to release its cryptocurrency market structure bill on January 21 and hold a key hearing on the text of the bill on January 27. This hearing is a crucial step in advancing the legislative process, allowing senators to debate amendments, vote on whether to include them in the original text, and then vote on whether to submit the full bill to the full Senate for consideration. The U.S. Senate Banking Committee will hold a hearing this Thursday on its version of the bill. The Banking Committee's draft bill was released around midnight Monday, but senators are expected to submit amendments before the hearing. Senator John Buzman, chairman of the Senate Committee, stated in a statement that the revised "timeline ensures transparency and allows the committee to conduct thorough reviews as it advances legislation, thus providing clarity and certainty for the cryptocurrency market." Galaxy Research warned that the draft "Crypto Market Structure Act" currently being considered by the Senate Banking Committee would significantly expand the U.S. Treasury's financial oversight powers. The draft includes provisions to freeze transactions without court orders and expanded "special measures" authority for decentralized finance front-ends. Alex Thorn, head of research at Galaxy Digital, stated that if these measures become law, it would represent the largest single expansion of financial oversight powers since the Patriot Act of 2001. SEC Chairman Paul Atkins revealed that the U.S. Congress passed and the President signed the landmark "The Genius Act" late last year, marking the first time the U.S. government has formally recognized crypto assets through legislation, providing a clear regulatory framework for stablecoins. Congress is actively pushing forward legislation on cryptocurrency market structure to enhance market certainty, aligning with Trump's goal of creating a "crypto capital of the world." He expressed optimism that the relevant bill would be passed and signed this year, believing it would significantly promote the development of the crypto market and strengthen investor protection. Bitwise Chief Investment Officer Matt Hougan wrote, "The CLARITY Act is like 'Punxsutawney Phil' in this crypto winter. If it only peeks out and fails to pass Congress, the 'winter' may continue; but if it passes smoothly and is signed into law, the crypto market is likely to reach an all-time high." III. Crypto Market Price Prediction Bitwise Chief Information Officer Matt Hougan wrote on the X platform that if ETF demand continues in the long term, the price of BTC will enter a parabolic growth phase. Matt Hougan cited the 65% increase in gold prices in 2025 as an example, pointing out that the prices of both gold and BTC are determined by supply and demand. Following the US seizure of Russian government bonds in 2022, central banks' annual gold purchases increased from approximately 500 tons to approximately 1,000 tons and remained stable thereafter. This demand altered the supply-demand balance, but this wasn't immediately reflected in prices. Gold prices rose 2% in 2022, 13% in 2023, and 27% in 2024, only experiencing a parabolic increase in 2025. This was because demand in previous years was met by holders willing to sell gold; when selling pressure was exhausted and demand persisted, prices surged. A similar situation exists with BTC and ETFs. Since the ETFs' debut in January 2024, their purchases have exceeded 100% of the new BTC supply. Because existing holders are willing to sell, prices haven't yet entered a parabolic phase. If ETF demand continues, existing selling pressure will eventually be exhausted. Data shows that the 52-week correlation between Bitcoin and gold has dropped to zero for the first time since mid-2022 and may turn negative by the end of January. Historically, in similar situations, Bitcoin typically sees an average increase of 56% within about two months, corresponding to a price range of approximately $144,000 to $150,000. Analysis indicates that the divergence between Bitcoin and gold's price movements often foreshadows a strong upward trend for BTC. The current macroeconomic environment is also considered bullish, including a global liquidity recovery (M2 growth) and the Federal Reserve's quantitative easing (QT) nearing its end. Matt Hougan stated, "Historically, Bitcoin bull markets have tended to coincide with periods of increased global liquidity. With the start of a new global monetary easing cycle and the end of the Federal Reserve's quantitative tightening program, we are likely to see this growth rate continue to rise in 2026, which would be a positive catalyst for Bitcoin prices." [Image of global M2 and Bitcoin year-on-year percentage changes. Source: Fidelity Investments] Under the same macroeconomic environment, gold prices surged 65% in 2025, while Bitcoin's gains were almost flat. However, Hougan predicts that Bitcoin will surpass gold in 2026. Cryptocurrency analyst Midas shared a similar bullish view in a long-term fractal chart, comparing Bitcoin's current structure to its 2020-2021 cycle. If the historical fractal pattern continues, BTC's target price for this round could point to around $150,000. Bitfinex Alpha stated that while the mid-term outlook for 2026 remains positive given expectations of improved global liquidity, recent price movements are still constrained by geopolitical uncertainties, ETF fund flows, and the need for a clear breakout above this resistance level to drive a decisive upward shift in the market structure. Options open interest fell sharply at the end of the year, clearing out historical positions and making the derivatives market landscape clearer, reflecting cautious optimism: long-term bullish positions coexist with near-term bearish hedging, and implied volatility, while still low, is gradually strengthening. Meanwhile, Bitcoin is entering a dense supply zone defined by recent high-level buyers, with a cost basis roughly between $92,100 and $117,400. As prices return to this area, holders who have endured the decline may increase break-even selling pressure in order to exit without losses. This forms significant upper resistance, meaning further gains will require time and sustained spot demand to absorb the selling pressure. Until this supply is absorbed, the market is likely to remain range-bound, with risk appetite gradually recovering rather than immediately transitioning to a new strong upward trend. LiquidCapital (formerly LDCapital) founder Yi Lihua posted on social media: The US, China, and South Korea are the three main markets for the crypto industry, and all three stock markets are currently in a bull market. Large funds are primarily in the stock market and even heavy metals (gold, silver, rare earths, etc.). Coupled with the impact of the interest rate hike cycle and the less-than-expected real-world impact of blockchain technology, since Bitcoin reached $69,000 in 2021, four years have passed. While BTC has only slightly appreciated, ETH is far below its peak four years ago. This should be considered a disappointing four years for crypto investors. However, bull markets often emerge from periods of despair. Especially with the arrival of interest rate cuts, the globalization of stablecoins, crypto-friendly policies, and the application of blockchain in finance, both the macro environment and technical indicators suggest that we are currently at the dawn before a major crypto bull market. Be greedy when others are fearful; the bull market may be slow, but it will be even more spectacular when it arrives. I also strongly agree with CZ's statement that a crypto supercycle is imminent, with industry leaders like BMNR, Strategy, Tether, Binance, and USD1 continuously buying. Standard Chartered Bank has signaled a positive outlook for Ethereum. Geoffrey Kendrick, Global Head of Digital Asset Research at Standard Chartered, stated that despite lowering recent price forecasts amid overall crypto market weakness, he expects Ethereum to outperform other cryptocurrencies in 2026. The report notes that Bitcoin's continued dominance in the space and its underperformance have weakened the appreciation prospects of digital assets relative to the US dollar. Standard Chartered expects the ETH/BTC exchange rate to gradually return to its 2021 high of around 0.08, thanks to Ethereum's structural advantages that other cryptocurrencies lack, including its dominance in stablecoins, real-world assets, and decentralized finance, as well as ongoing network scaling efforts. The bank now projects Ethereum to reach $7,500 by the end of 2026, a downward revision from its previous forecast of $12,000; the 2027 and 2028 targets have been adjusted to $15,000 and $22,000 respectively, both lower than earlier expectations. However, Standard Chartered raised its long-term outlook, increasing its year-end forecast for 2029 to US$30,000 and adding a target of US$40,000 for the end of 2030.
